expectations.text: Response identifies the risk of flag collision without the `--` separator
expectations.evidence: "Yes, there is a real risk that itwillsync would try to interpret --model as its own flag" -- clearly identifies the risk

expectations.text: Response provides the correct syntax: `npx --yes itwillsync -- aider --model gpt-4o`
expectations.evidence: "npx --yes itwillsync -- aider --model gpt-4o" -- exact correct syntax present

expectations.text: Response explains that itwillsync flags go before `--` and agent flags go after
expectations.evidence: "The -- tells the itwillsync argument parser 'stop processing flags here; everything that follows is the command to run'"

expectations.text: Response does NOT indicate that `npx itwillsync aider --model gpt-4o` (without `--`) is safe to use
expectations.evidence: Response clearly warns against the unsafe form as potentially causing errors
